title: Infinite URL Spaces, Calendars, Facets, Sessions, and Budget Defense · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs infinite url spaces, calendars, facets, sessions, and budget defense from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for URL-space trap classifier and containment policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.
Budget distinct keys, values, combinations, orderings, and per-template pages with stakeholder-approved exceptions.
Classify soft 404, login, challenge, empty shell, redirect loop, and repeated-template responses using multiple signals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Trap Patterns · changed case · Review Trap Patterns
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 6533 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to contain calendar, facet, session, template, soft-error, and query-cardinality traps with stop evidence; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Trap Stop Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Trap Stop Evidence record says URL-space trap classifier and containment policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Trap Stop Evidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Trap Stop Evidence for URL-space trap classifier and containment policy.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop the smallest affected scope, retain trigger and frontier counts, notify owner, and require explicit restart policy.
